data.title: Zebra upgraded on improving market demand: Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Zebra Technologies (NASDAQ:ZBRA) was upgraded to Equal-Weight from Underweight by Morgan Stanley due to improving market demand over the next twelve months and a growing customer base.

The price target was also increased by more than 30% to $400 from $305. Zebra shares have steadily climbed throughout the year, gaining more than 70% over the past 12 months. Morgan Stanley finds that Zebra's current valuation of $407 is slightly ahead of the recovery.

Zebra is a mobile computing company based in Lincolnshire, Ill., a northern suburb of Chicago.

"With our latest refresh of our retail/distribution capex tracker improving and consumer survey data points turning more positive, we no longer see a negative catalyst and view next twelve months risk-reward as more balanced," said Morgan Stanley analysts Meta Marshall and Karan Juvekar. "In order to get more positive, we would like to see ZBRA approach high single-digit growth rates (i.e. outperform 5-7% long-term targets), which requires inflection in non-core segments like RFID or machine vision, not contemplated in our base case."

Marshall also noted that Zebra was seeing large North American projects increase during the third quarter, which are expected to continue through the fourth quarter as well.

"We believe there is potential for a meaningful refresh cycle in 2025 for customers that built out capacity during the pandemic, as typical mobile computer refresh cycles are 4-5 years in nature, though we are still cautious on degree of opportunity," he added.

Seeking Alpha analyst Hunter Wolf Research recently gave Zebra a Buy rating and set a $450 12-month price target on the stock.

Zebra management guides for 28% to 31% revenue growth during the fourth quarter and projects $850M in free cash flow for fiscal year 2024, which highlights strong end-market recovery.

However, Hunter Wolf noted nearly 50% of finished goods production is based in China, and if tariffs are installed, it could affect margins in the near term.

data.title: U.S. Physical Therapy buys eight-clinic hand and physical therapy practice
data.description:
• U.S. Physical Therapy (NYSE:USPH) has acquired a 75% stake in a hand and physical therapy practice with eight clinic locations in Wyoming.
  • The current owners retain a 25% ownership interest. The practice currently generates approximately $6.5M in annual revenues and 43,000 in annual visits.

data.title: Novocure therapy succeeds in pancreatic cancer trial
data.description:
Novocure (NASDAQ:NVCR) has met the primary endpoint of overall survival in a late-stage trial evaluating the use of its Tumor Treating Fields (TTFields) therapy for pancreatic cancer.

Shares of the oncology company jumped 25% premarket on Monday.

The pivotal, Phase 3 PANOVA-3 trial met its primary endpoint, demonstrating a statistically significant improvement in median overall survival versus control.

The study is designed to test the efficacy and safety of TTFields therapy used concomitantly with gemcitabine and nab-paclitaxel, as a first-line treatment of locally advanced pancreatic adenocarcinoma.

Patients were randomized to receive either TTFields therapy concomitant with gemcitabine and nab-paclitaxel or gemcitabine and nab-paclitaxel alone.

Novocure (NVCR) plans to file for regulatory approval of TTFields in unresectable, locally advanced pancreatic adenocarcinoma based on PANOVA-3.

The company is also exploring the use of TTFields therapy together with atezolizumab, gemcitabine and nab-paclitaxel for the treatment of metastatic pancreatic cancer in a Phase 2 PANOVA-4 trial, with data anticipated in 2026.

data.title: Johnson & Johnson seeks approval of pediatric indications for TREMFYA
data.description:
• Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) submitted two supplemental Biologics License Applications to the U.S. FDA seeking approval of TREMFYA for treating children 6 years and older with moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis (PsO) and children 5 years of age and older with active juvenile psoriatic arthritis.
  • The submission is based on data from the late stage study in pediatric patients with moderate to severe plaque PsO and bridging pharmacokinetic data from the Phase 3 VOYAGE 1 and 2 studies.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bausch Health names Jonathan Sadeh as chief medical officer
data.description:
• Bausch Health Companies (NYSE:BHC) said it has appointed Jonathan Sadeh as its chief medical officer.
  • Sadeh has over 20 years of expertise, and most recently at Bristol-Meyers Squibb, he was responsible for multiple drug approvals in dermatology and gastroenterology.

data.title: MicroStrategy bought ~$1.5B in bitcoin from Nov. 25-Dec. 1
data.description:
• MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) purchased 15,400 bitcoins for about $1.5B in cash during the period between Nov. 25 and Dec. 1, the company said in a filing.
  • The purchases were made using proceeds from the company’s recent $21B stock offering.
  • As of Dec. 1, approximately $11.3B of shares remained available for sale pursuant to the sales deal.
  • The company held an aggregate of about 402.1K bitcoin as of Dec. 1, which were acquired at an aggregate purchase price of around $23.4B.

data.title: Nano Labs plans to buy and hold $50M worth bitcoin in five years
data.description:
• Nano Labs (NASDAQ:NA) said it plans to purchase and hold $50 million worth of bitcoin (BTC-USD) over the next five years.
  • This comes amid more and more companies looking to invest in the cryptocurrency as BTC has been hovering around $100K, boosted by Donald Trump's win in the U.S. elections.
  • "By incorporating BTC into its strategic asset portfolio, Nano Labs aims to strengthen its market position and contribute to the broader adoption and growth of the digital economy," the company said.

data.title: Applied Digital closes $150M senior secured note With Macquarie Equipment Capital
data.description:
• Digital infrastructure solutions and cloud services provider Applied Digital (NASDAQ:APLD) and its unit has entered into a promissory note with Macquarie Equipment Capital for a loan of $150M.
  • APLD shares were trading +0.79% Monday pre-market at $10.18.
  • The promissory note, which is expected to carry an interest of 0.25% per annum, was fully drawn on Nov. 27.
  • The promissory note is expected to mature on the earlier of the date of acceleration of the loan or May 27, 2026.
  • The proceeds will be used, in part, to prepay obligations.
  • As partial consideration for the loan, APLD issued to the lender warrants to purchase up to 1.04M shares of common stock in a private placement. The warrants will have an exercise price of $9.66 per share.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: PG&E raises dividend by 150% to $0.025
data.description:
• PG&E (NYSE:PCG) declares $0.025/share quarterly dividend, 150% increase from prior dividend of $0.010.
  • Payable Jan. 15; for shareholders of record Dec. 31; ex-div Dec. 31.
  • See PCG Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Okta, Cloudflare upgraded at Morgan Stanley; SentinelOne, Tenable downgraded
data.description:
Morgan Stanley made several ratings changes to cybersecurity stocks on Monday, upgrading Okta (NASDAQ:OKTA) and Cloudflare (NYSE:NET), and downgrading SentinelOne (NYSE:S) and Tenable (NASDAQ:TENB).

Okta and Cloudflare shares rose 4.5% and 3.5% in premarket trading, while SentinelOne and Tenable were off around 2% each.

Okta

Okta was raised to Overweight from Equal-Weight, as the firm's analysts said they see the demand environment "stabilizing," the headwinds from competition appear to be easing and new products are taking hold. "Our latest reseller checks support this view, pointing to improving demand and net share gain vs competitors," the analysts wrote. "Further, our channel conversations have skewed positively for OIG (Okta Identity Governance), and we believe this offering could reach $100M in ACV by Q4." The analysts also upped their price target to $97 from $92 on Okta.

Cloudflare

The analysts upped their rating on Cloudflare to Overweight from Equal-Weight as the firm believes there is a path to "sustainable" 25% to 30% revenue growth over the next few years, stemming from multiple product cycles, growing contribution of artificial intelligence at the edge and a fully ramped enterprise sales force.

"We think the stock sustains a premium valuation on higher estimates as growth remains stronger for longer," the analysts wrote.

SentinelOne

SentinelOne was cut to Equal-Weight from Overweight as the firm believes there will be "slower expected demand" for the core endpoint security market in 2025, along with a challenging competitive and pricing environment and concerns that its margins will remain "well below" its peers on higher go-to-market and R&D spending.

"Additionally, while the sales org has stabilized under new [go-to-market] leadership, MSCO thinks execution risks are not fully discounted at these levels and see modest downside to consensus CY25/26 [annual recurring revenue] forecasts," the analysts wrote in a note.

Tenable

Tenable was also downgraded to Equal-Weight from Overweight (and its price target trimmed to $47 from $50) as the firm also said it sees pricing pressures in the vulnerability management market. Other concerns include its "significant" exposure to the U.S. public sector and "limited upside" to margins.

"While valuation at 5x EV/CY26 Sales and 18x on [free cash flow] doesn't appear demanding vs peers at 5.4x and 20.5x, respectively, MSCO believes slowing growth and downside to forward [free cash flow] estimates keeps TENB's valuation range bound," the analysts wrote.

In addition, Morgan Stanley also tweaked price targets on CrowdStrike (CRWD) and CyberArk (CYBR) to $390 and $316 from $355 and $295, respectively. The firm also raised its price target on Fortinet (FTNT) to $113 from $106.
